prompt: Create a runtime prop sprite for a fantasy tactics RPG: a single closed reliquary chest intended to read clearly at very small size with a much more pixelated look.
subject: one closed chapel chest with a low broad silhouette, dark oak body, forged iron bands, worn brass lockplate, restrained crimson cloth trim, and simple ecclesiastical ornament
style: chunky pixel-art fantasy tactics prop sprite, large pixel clusters, simplified shading, crisp silhouette, old-world moody fantasy
composition: single object centered, three-quarter view, low and wide proportions, tight crop, no floor plane
lighting: simple warm top light, restrained contrast, no cast shadow, no glow
palette: weathered oak brown, charcoal iron, aged brass, muted crimson cloth, ember-warm highlights
materials: worn wood, forged iron, scuffed brass, slightly frayed cloth trim
constraints: this sprite will be rendered around 58 pixels wide on a 96x48 isometric tile, so prioritize big blocky readable shapes, reduced detail, limited palette feel, and crisp silhouette after downscaling; keep it broad rather than tall; transparent background; no extra objects
negative: no photorealism, no glossy 3D render, no baked floor shadow, no thin filigree, no micro-detail clutter, no painterly blur, no soft airbrushing, no dramatic camera angle, no text, no extra props, no oversized empty padding
